title: pedicularis roylei var. brevigaleata
intro: Pedicularis roylei var. brevigaleata, also known as the Himalayan lousewort, is a rare and lesser-known species of flowering plant in the balsam family. It is used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation, treating respiratory issues, and acting as an antiseptic, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness. Herbal preparations of Pedicularis roylei var. brevigaleata typically involve drying and infusing the leaves and flowers in hot water to make a tea, although commercial preparations may be available in some regions.
